hero.title: 3 Days in Hyderabad.
hero.titleAccent: Pearls, Palaces and the Best Biryani in India.
hero.intro: The City of Pearls earns its nickname. Hyderabad blends Nizami grandeur, Charminar's old-city buzz, and a food culture built around the most famous biryani in the country. Three days, properly spent.
hero.heroImageAlt: Charminar Hyderabad old city at dusk

overview.heading: 3 Days Hyderabad Heritage & Culture Tour: The Full Picture
body: Hyderabad is one of India's great underrated city breaks. The cultural heart of Telangana carries the legacy of the Nizams, once among the richest rulers on earth, in its palaces, its pearl trade and its extraordinary kitchens. The old city around Charminar still hums with the same energy it has for centuries.

This 3 day Hyderabad tour package from Malaysia runs privately, with your own vehicle and driver guide. You will see the iconic Charminar, the mighty Golconda Fort, the serene Hussain Sagar lake, and shop for the famous Hyderabadi pearls, all on a schedule you control.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Charminar & the Old City
items.body: The 16th-century Charminar is Hyderabad's defining monument, surrounded by the bustling Laad Bazaar where bangles, pearls and perfumes have been traded for generations. Come hungry.

items.title: Golconda Fort
items.body: A vast hilltop fortress famous for its acoustics and the legend of the Koh-i-Noor diamond, which was once traded here. The climb rewards you with sweeping views over the city.

items.title: Pearls & the Famous Biryani
items.body: Hyderabad has been India's pearl capital for centuries. Pair a shopping trip with a plate of Hyderabadi dum biryani, slow-cooked and unrivalled. Your guide knows the legendary kitchens.

days.title: Arrive Hyderabad
days.description: Met at Rajiv Gandhi International Airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening free, perhaps a first biryani near your hotel.

days.title: Full Day Hyderabad Sightseeing
days.description: Your private vehicle and guide cover the city's heritage highlights at a relaxed pace.

days.title: Free Morning, Depart
days.description: Checkout at noon. Last shopping or a lakeside stroll before your airport transfer.

whyBook.heading: South India Heritage, Handled Locally
whyBook.body: Hyderabad's old city is wonderful but dense, and the heritage sites are spread out. A private guide threads it all together and gets you to the right biryani. Dhesu has run South India tours since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Hyderabad Tour Package from Malaysia: City of Pearls
sections.body:
Hyderabad is a brilliant short break for travellers who want heritage, shopping and food without the crowds of the bigger tourist trails. The Nizami legacy gives the city a unique character, grand yet down to earth.

This private package means you cover Charminar, Golconda and the pearl markets at your own pace, with a guide who knows the old city's rhythm and the best places to eat.

Want a slightly longer version? Our 4 day Hyderabad Heritage Escape adds more time for the palaces and shopping.

sections.heading: What to Buy in Hyderabad
sections.body:
Pearls top the list. Hyderabad has been India's pearl trading centre for centuries, with reputable dealers offering everything from simple strands to elaborate sets at competitive prices.

Laad Bazaar near Charminar is famous for lac bangles studded with stones, a Hyderabadi specialty. Attars (traditional perfumes) and Bidri metalware are other distinctive buys.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Hyderabad?
items.answer: October to February is the most comfortable, with mild, dry weather ideal for sightseeing. March to May gets hot. The monsoon (June to September) is moderate. Winter is the ideal window for exploring the old city and forts.

items.question: Is Hyderabad safe and easy for Malaysian tourists?
items.answer: Yes. Hyderabad is a large, cosmopolitan city used to visitors, with widespread English and a strong food and shopping culture. Standard travel precautions apply, and your driver guide handles local navigation.

items.question: Can I get halal food in Hyderabad?
items.answer: Easily. Hyderabad has a large Muslim community and the city's signature biryani culture is deeply rooted in it. Halal restaurants and biryani houses are everywhere, particularly around the old city and Charminar.
